SOCCER REPORT | 2026-05-21

HEADLINE
Slumping Austin FC fires coach, sporting director before World Cup break

SNAPSHOT
Soccer board refreshed with 19 news items, 0 live matches, 3 post-match reads and 0 upcoming items. the coverage is prioritizing interpretation over match-result listings, with attention on world cup 2026 runway, manager or coaching pressure, availability or suspension watch, star player signal.

- Slumping Austin FC fires coach, sporting director before World Cup break
- Germany recall Manuel Neuer out of retirement for World Cup squad

- Arteta: It is one of the best feelings I've ever had

- Sources: Miami closing in on signing Man United's Casemiro
- Want to hire the next Jürgen Klopp? This is how data can help clubs pick a manager

NEWSROOM READ
- Slumping Austin FC fires coach, sporting director before World Cup break: Slumping Austin FC fired head coach Nico Estévez and sporting director Rodolfo Borrell on Monday as the team sits near the bottom of the MLS standings just one season after returning to the playoffs.
- Germany recall Manuel Neuer out of retirement for World Cup squad: Veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer has been coaxed out of retirement for the World Cup by Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann.
- Ranked: Top 50 USMNT players for World Cup, based on club form: Who are the 50 best Americans who should be selected for the World Cup roster? We look at club form and rank them as the USMNT Player Performance Index returns.
- Arteta: It is one of the best feelings I've ever had: Mikel Arteta reveals the moment he found out that Arsenal had won the Premier League and says Arsenal must continue to 'reinvent' themselves ahead of the Champions League final. The table context matters here: title pressure, European qualification, relegation risk and fixture congestion can all change how this story is read.
- Masked Modric back to aid Pulisic, Milan's Champions League push: AC Milan will be looking to a masked superhero as the battle for the Champions League spots goes down to the last day in Serie A. The table context matters here: title pressure, European qualification, relegation risk and fixture congestion can all change how this story is read.
